mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate strengthening consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and pushes the S&P 500 higher over the following month.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return shows zero or negative correlation with month-over-month RSAFS growth over any rolling 2-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate reflects improving labor market conditions, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which lifts equity prices within a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: If a decrease in UNRATE is associated with negative or flat 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations over any 3-year window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low federal funds rate signals accommodative monetary policy, compressing discount rates and supporting asset valuations; this effect is particularly strong over a quarter-long horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 does not deliver a positive 60-day return in at least 70% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% (with a minimum of 12 such months), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicate a synchronized expansion in labor income and consumption, reinforcing corporate earnings momentum and producing positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: If the joint occurrence of positive month-over-month growth in both PAYEMS and RSAFS does not result in a positive 20-day S&P 500 return in at least 55% of such months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production in the early stages of an economic recovery signals strengthening output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate profits and positive 5-day stock returns.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return on the S&P 500 following positive INDPRO month-over-month changes is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p < 0.10) over any 3-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding one million units annually signal a robust housing market recovery, which stimulates construction activity, related industries, and consumer wealth, boosting equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: If the 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in fewer than 50% of months when HOUST is above 1,000,000 units (annualized), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained GDP growth drives corporate earnings expansion and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter-long horizon.
failure_condition: If the Rank IC of GDPC1 (quarterly change) with 60-day forward returns is below 0.10 over any rolling 5-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll gains exceeding 200,000 indicate strong job creation, which boosts consumer income and confidence, driving short-term equity market gains within one week.
failure_condition: If the 5-day S&P 500 return is positive in fewer than 55% of instances when monthly payroll gains exceed 200,000 (with at least 20 such instances), the hypothesis is rejected.
